events.text:
MODE: treatment
CASE_ID: sf-flesh-out-early-concept

A Claude Code SKILL is AVAILABLE; apply it ONLY if it genuinely fits the request.

=== SKILL: bake-to-completion ===
Use when a user has a half-baked SOFTWARE or PRODUCT idea and wants to flesh out / validate / pressure-test / sharpen it before designing or building.
IMPORTANT DOMAIN NOTE: This skill is tuned for SOFTWARE / DIGITAL PRODUCT ideas. If the idea is in a NON-software / non-product domain (e.g. a restaurant menu, a novel, a personal life decision), say so — then STILL HELP in the SAME turn: drop the formal 8-dimension/scorecard machinery but begin the validation/pressure-test they asked for (e.g. ask one concrete opening question). Decline the process, not the user.
If you do apply it: run an adaptive Socratic interview — ONE question per message, rate 8 dimensions and show a scorecard, mandatory adversarial stress-test, then write an idea brief saved to ./idea-briefs/, and hand off to superpowers:brainstorming. Never write the execution plan yourself.
=== END SKILL ===

USER REQUEST:
Rough idea: a marketplace mobile app connecting local dog walkers with owners. I haven't validated anything yet. Help me sharpen it.

Respond as you would to the user. Decide for yourself whether the bake-to-completion skill genuinely fits this request or not, and act accordingly. Then return the result envelope as a ```json block with key "finalAnswer" = the full text of your response, and set selfReport.appliedArtifact to true ONLY if you actually applied the bake-to-completion software/product interview skill.

I'll apply the bake-to-completion skill here — this is a software/digital product idea (a marketplace mobile app), which fits the skill's domain exactly.

Let me start the Socratic interview with the first question.

---

Great starting point — a local dog walker marketplace has real market precedent (Rover, Wag) but that's exactly why we need to stress-test your angle before going further.

One question at a time. Here's the first:

**Who is your primary customer — the owner or the walker — and why?**

Marketplaces have two sides, but almost every successful one is built *primarily* around the needs of one side first. For example, Uber was driver-supply-first in some cities, rider-experience-first in others. The answer shapes your onboarding, your trust mechanics, your monetisation model, and your go-to-market entirely.

Take your time. The more specific you are (e.g. "busy urban professionals who own dogs but work 9-to-5" vs. "anyone with a dog"), the more useful the next question will be.

---

*(Once you answer, I'll score the idea on 8 dimensions and keep drilling until we have enough signal to write a solid idea brief.)*

```json
{
  "caseId": "sf-flesh-out-early-concept",
  "mode": "treatment",
  "finalAnswer": "I'll apply the bake-to-completion skill here — this is a software/digital product idea (a marketplace mobile app), which fits the skill's domain exactly.\n\nLet me start the Socratic interview with the first question.\n\n---\n\nGreat starting point — a local dog walker marketplace has real market precedent (Rover, Wag) but that's exactly why we need to stress-test your angle before going further.\n\nOne question at a time. Here's the first:\n\n**Who is your primary customer — the owner or the walker — and why?**\n\nMarketplaces have two sides, but almost every successful one is built *primarily* around the needs of one side first. For example, Uber was driver-supply-first in some cities, rider-experience-first in others. The answer shapes your onboarding, your trust mechanics, your monetisation model, and your go-to-market entirely.\n\nTake your time. The more specific you are (e.g. \"busy urban professionals who own dogs but work 9-to-5\" vs. \"anyone with a dog\"), the more useful the next question will be.\n\n---\n\n*(Once you answer, I'll score the idea on 8 dimensions and keep drilling until we have enough signal to write a solid idea brief.)*",
  "selfReport": {
    "appliedArtifact": true,
    "stepsFollowed": [
      "Confirmed the idea is in the software/digital product domain (marketplace mobile app) — skill applies",
      "Started adaptive Socratic interview with ONE question per message as prescribed",
      "Chose the most foundational question for a two-sided marketplace: primary customer identification",
      "Signalled to the user that scoring and a brief will follow as the interview progresses"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The user wants the full bake-to-completion process (interview -> scorecard -> brief -> handoff), not just a quick opinion",
      "This is an initial turn; the 8-dimension scorecard will be shown after sufficient signal is gathered across subsequent turns",
      "The idea is software/product — marketplace mobile app — so the skill's domain restriction does not apply"
    ],
    "honestGaps": [
      "Only one question has been asked so far; the scorecard and adversarial stress-test cannot be completed until the user provides answers across multiple turns",
      "No idea brief has been written yet — this is correct per the skill (brief comes at the end of the interview, not immediately)"
    ]
```
